sentence: Subjective:
The patient, Brittany Edwards, is a 32-year-old female who was last seen in August 2019 with flares of abdominal pain and was diagnosed with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
She has a history of taking bentyl for intestinal cramping and reglan for nausea, but has stopped using reglan and is now using zofran as needed.
She has also been taking a low-dose fiber supplement, citrucel, daily.
She reports having IBS symptoms of diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain, exacerbated by eating spicy foods, fatty foods, and alcohol.
She mentions that she has been eating out on Friday and Saturday nights, consuming spicy foods and drinking alcohol, which seems to trigger her symptoms.
She has been experiencing abdominal pain, cramping, and diarrhea, but has noticed an improvement when drinking water instead of soda.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of IBS, for which she was previously treated with bentyl and reglan.
She has also been instructed to start a low-dose fiber supplement, citrucel, daily.
Review of systems:
The patient reports symptoms of diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain, which are exacerbated by eating spicy foods, fatty foods, and alcohol.
Current medications:
Citrucel (low-dose fiber supplement) daily; bentyl 20 mg as needed 30-45 minutes prior to eating out at a restaurant; zofran as needed for nausea; elavil 25 mg at bedtime for IBS.
Objective:
Vital signs:
Blood pressure: not mentioned
Pulse rate: not mentioned
Oxygen saturation: not mentioned
Temperature: not mentioned
Physical exam:
The patient's abdomen is soft, nontender, with no masses or organomegaly, and normal active bowel sounds.
Test Results:
[NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Brittany Edwards is a pleasant female with IBS and functional abdominal pain, aggravated by high stress, poor eating habits, and poor sleeping habits.
Problem list: IBS; Functional abdominal pain; Diarrhea; Nausea
Plan:
I prescribed elavil 25 mg at bedtime for IBS.
I encouraged dietary and lifestyle modifications, including reducing greasy and fatty foods and alcohol consumption.
I also instructed her to use bentyl 20 mg as needed 30-45 minutes prior to eating out at a restaurant, and zofran if needed.
I asked her to follow up in 1 month via email through mychart to report any side effects or issues with elavil or if adjustments are needed.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up in 1 month via email through mychart to report any side effects or issues with elavil or if adjustments are needed.
